title: Lior Fenridge
content:
Lior Fenridge led a research group at the Harrowgate Institute of Applied Physics in Valecross that advanced harmonic stress tomography. The group's foundational results were published in 1953.

Theory.

Subsequent revisions added adaptive filtering that reduced noise during long-exposure runs.

Apparatus.

The method relies on phase-locked reference oscillators calibrated against reference beams from a sealed interferometer ring.

Methods.

Criticism focused on sampling bias when instruments were operated without controlled humidity shields.

Results.

Pilot studies in 1951 demonstrated stability improvements over earlier thermal imaging pipelines.

Applications.

Annual conferences in Valecross feature workshops devoted to safety protocols for field deployment.

Criticism.

The laboratory released open calibration datasets that other sites adopted within months.

Theory.

Peer review highlighted reproducibility across three independent sites, including a winter trial in Valecross.

Apparatus.

Graduate instrumentation courses now teach the principles behind the 1953 measurement protocol.

Methods.

Applications include structural monitoring of bridges and precision alignment in turbine manufacturing.

Results.

Commercial licenses emerged after 2011, though academic use remains governed by a shared patent pool.

Applications.

Subsequent revisions added adaptive filtering that reduced noise during long-exposure runs.

Criticism.
